jokes.text: the mad dash at the end of the night when guys are really scrambling to get some booty? And it's like a game, and the shot clock is running out. It's like football or something.
jokes.setup: describing the frantic end-of-night pickup attempts
jokes.punchline: comparing it to sports with a shot clock
jokes.explanation: Solid observational comedy with a fresh sports metaphor. Sets up the upcoming sketch premise well.

jokes.text: left guard, Carol Deitrich, and right guard, Denisha 'Bear' Bryant
jokes.setup: Documentary-style narration about a pickup attempt
jokes.punchline: Treating the woman's friends like literal football defensive positions with player names
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of the sports metaphor with specific football terminology and character names

jokes.text: a tandem that had racked up 14 cock blocks just a year earlier
jokes.setup: describing the defensive line protecting the woman
jokes.punchline: using football stats terminology for cock blocking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant merger of sports commentary with sexual terminology. The specificity of '14 cock blocks' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You give me Pac-Man fever. (gobbling)
jokes.setup: Bobby trying to win over Gina with video game references
jokes.punchline: the absurd pickup line with accompanying sound effects
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully awkward pickup line that perfectly captures bad 80s game references. The gobbling sound adds physical comedy.

jokes.text: shots up the gut
jokes.setup: continuing the football metaphor for pickup attempts
jokes.punchline: sexual double entendre disguised as football terminology
jokes.explanation: Effective double entendre that maintains the sports commentary conceit while being sexually suggestive.

jokes.text: You know, I got some pizzas at the crib. All right. Pizza? I don't want no pizza. And some weed, too. Weed? A'ight, yeah.
jokes.punchline: adding weed instantly changes everyone's mind
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke showing how priorities instantly shift. The quick reversal from all parties is expertly timed.

jokes.text: That night, Bobby and Gina had drunken sex on top of her two passed-out friends, technically making it a four-way.
jokes.setup: expecting a romantic conclusion to the pickup story
jokes.punchline: the crude reality with twisted logic about what constitutes a foursome
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subversive ending that takes the sports metaphor to its logical crude conclusion. The word 'technically' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I look like Turbo from Breakin'. I really got into that character, too. 'Cause after we was done shooting, I cleaned up the set.
jokes.setup: commenting on his retro costume in the sketch
jokes.punchline: taking method acting too far by actually cleaning the set
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary that shows Dave's commitment to character while being absurd. The Breakin' reference dates it perfectly.

jokes.text: you know any real robber would've shot the little dude in the first five minutes, roll credits, the end
jokes.setup: talking about unrealistic movie scenarios in Home Alone
jokes.punchline: the brutal reality of what would actually happen
jokes.explanation: Perfectly sets up the 'Real Movies' concept with dark but honest observation about movie logic vs reality.

jokes.text: (farting)
jokes.punchline: Neo farts during the dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor that deflates the Matrix tension. Works for the concept but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Psyche! It's Earl, nigga, I need your stapler. I can't find mine.
jokes.punchline: it's just a coworker needing office supplies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that perfectly deflates the Matrix mystique. The mundane stapler request is perfectly anticlimactic.

jokes.text: Hello, dumb-dumb, do you know who this is?
jokes.punchline: mocking the previous dramatic phone call format
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that extends the joke by mocking the original Matrix line structure.

jokes.text: Cream, get the money, dollar-dollar bill, y'all.
jokes.punchline: quoting Wu-Tang's C.R.E.A.M. in a corporate context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept introduction - using Wu-Tang lyrics in a legitimate financial commercial context.

jokes.text: Come step to the Wu, Wu-Tang Financial.
jokes.punchline: Wu-Tang as an actual financial services company
jokes.explanation: Perfect fusion of Wu-Tang aesthetic with corporate financial services. The name alone is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Smith Barney... bunch of bitches. Old-time farts.
jokes.punchline: dismissing competitors with Wu-Tang attitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice - exactly how Wu-Tang would dismiss traditional Wall Street firms.

jokes.text: You need to diversify your bonds, nigga.
jokes.punchline: using Wu-Tang slang to deliver sound investment wisdom
jokes.explanation: Absolutely iconic line that became a meme. Perfect merger of legitimate financial advice with Wu-Tang delivery.

jokes.text: Military shit is blowin' up. We about to go to war, invest in some nuclear bombs.
jokes.punchline: suggesting investment in weapons with casual tone
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny investment advice that feels authentic to Wu-Tang's aggressive style while being absurd.

jokes.text: This ain't Trading Places, nigga. This is real fucking life!
jokes.setup: emphasizing the seriousness of their financial services
jokes.punchline: contrasting with a comedy movie about finance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that distinguishes their hardcore approach from Hollywood depictions.

jokes.text: Protect your goddamn neck, a'ight?
jokes.punchline: using Wu-Tang song title as financial planning slogan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of Wu-Tang lyric as financial advice. Works on multiple levels - protecting yourself financially and the song reference.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, the Ol' Dirty Bastard couldn't make it today, but he sends his regards: Doo-da-doo-da-doo-da.
jokes.punchline: ODB's nonsensical vocal ad-lib as professional greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - ODB's random vocals translated into a professional context is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Wu-Tang Clan ain't nothin' to fuck with.
jokes.punchline: using Wu-Tang's most famous lyric as a financial services tagline
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that transforms an aggressive rap lyric into a corporate confidence statement.

jokes.text: Okay. You've gotta get the fuck outta here.
jokes.punchline: harsh reality of pimp immediately kicking her out
jokes.explanation: Effective subversion of Pretty Woman expectations. Quick, brutal reality check that ends the fantasy.

jokes.text: That happy nigga that's in that movie where he heals all white people, the big black runaway.
jokes.punchline: describing The Green Mile in sarcastically reductive terms
jokes.explanation: Sharp criticism disguised as description. Mooney's perspective on racial dynamics in Hollywood is incisive.

jokes.text: well, we don't do that, sell it to the niggers. They shoulda had somethin' to say about that.
jokes.punchline: calling out casual racism in classic films
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but necessary commentary on accepted racism in beloved films. Mooney's specialty.

jokes.text: That's funny, Stephen King. He almost said, 'nigger,' I read between 'em.
jokes.setup: responding to a viewer question from Stephen King
jokes.punchline: accusing the horror writer of racial undertones
jokes.explanation: Typical Mooney paranoia played for laughs while making serious points about racial subtext.

jokes.text: I wrote a script for Stephen King. I have a Stephen King horror movie... Nigger With A Brain. We'll see how that scare people. Niggers In School, how 'bout that, Stephen?
jokes.setup: talking about writing for Stephen King
jokes.punchline: proposing racially provocative horror titles
jokes.explanation: Provocative comedy that flips racial fears. The 'Niggers In School' reference to integration is particularly sharp.

jokes.text: And I mean bad like Catholic priest, R. Kelly bad.
jokes.setup: introducing a segment about bad sex
jokes.punchline: comparing to notorious sexual scandals
jokes.explanation: Effective setup that establishes the dark tone. The comparison anchors expectations for the upcoming sketch.

jokes.text: Know about sex with boys, Yoda did not. Tired Yoda is. Resign he will.
jokes.setup: Yoda being questioned about Jedi abuse scandal
jokes.punchline: maintaining his backwards speech pattern while denying knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Yoda's distinctive speech pattern makes the denial funnier and more absurd.

jokes.text: Yoda that was not. Then who was it? Mickey Rooney, maybe?
jokes.setup: Yoda denying he was on the incriminating tape
jokes.punchline: suggesting Mickey Rooney as an alternative
jokes.explanation: Absurd deflection that works because Mickey Rooney is such a random choice for comparison to Yoda.

jokes.text: Appalling Yoda's behavior was. Damn it! Now that freak has me talking like him.
jokes.setup: news anchor trying to maintain professionalism
jokes.punchline: realizing he's caught Yoda's speech pattern like a virus
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about how Yoda's speech is contagious. The anchor's frustration makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yes, they deserve to die, and I hope they burn in hell!
jokes.setup: being asked about punishment for Jedi criminals
jokes.punchline: delivering Samuel L. Jackson's famous Pulp Fiction line
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of Samuel L. Jackson's most famous line in a Star Wars context. The intensity is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: My master said the force was strong in me... and gave me a wine cooler.
jokes.setup: setting up typical Jedi training story
jokes.punchline: the wine cooler as grooming tactic
jokes.explanation: Dark turn that subverts Jedi mythology. Wine cooler choice makes it seem more pathetic than threatening.

jokes.text: Then he made me wear a blindfold and reach into his pants to... 'look for the force.'
jokes.punchline: using Jedi training language for sexual abuse
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but fits the sketch's premise. The Force terminology twist is predictable but necessary.

jokes.text: Are you sighing, or are you just... breathing normally? I don't even know anymore!
jokes.punchline: victim's confusion about normal vs. Darth Vader breathing
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to Darth Vader's breathing that shows long-term psychological damage in a funny way.

jokes.text: Point it out. (breathing heavily)
jokes.setup: being asked to show where he was touched on a doll
jokes.punchline: the heavy breathing getting more pronounced and disturbing
jokes.explanation: The breathing escalation works for the Vader reference but feels somewhat cheap given the subject matter.

jokes.text: Can we cut tape? Miguel, you heard the man. Cut the goddamn tape! Just cut it!
jokes.punchline: anchor desperately trying to end the segment
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable moment that feels realistic for how a news anchor would react to this situation.

jokes.text: This never would've happened in the world of Star Trek 'cause the fleet commanders would not have allowed... you can't prove that! (screaming)
jokes.punchline: argument escalating to screaming over sci-fi morality
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing how geeks would react to fictional scandal. The escalation feels authentic.

jokes.text: gay droid marriage, should it be legalized? This queer couple says yes.
jokes.punchline: applying contemporary political issues to Star Wars universe
jokes.explanation: Decent extension of the Star Wars news parody into contemporary political territory.

jokes.text: I'm rich, bi-atch! (horn honking)
jokes.punchline: Dave's signature catchphrase with car horn
jokes.explanation: Classic Chappelle Show ending. While not original to this episode, it became iconic and perfectly caps the show.
